name: cabin-witch
summary: A hedge-witch's one-room cabin — drying herbs crowd the rafters, a cauldron bubbles somewhere, and the walls keep secrets.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: near-square or slightly elongated around the hearth

primary_palette.note: classic brewing witch — the cauldron is the altar
apothecary witch — precise, recipe-driven, rural healer
devotional witch — the cabin is a shrine to an old spirit
kitchen-witch — magic is cookery and cookery is magic
summoner — the working is mid-ritual, do not cross the line

story_prompts: occupied, the witch stirs the pot — do not interrupt the brewing
empty and dusty — she has been gone a full moon, something in the pot has started speaking
the working went wrong — chalk circle broken, candles burned black, a cold wind from the hearth
the village healer lives here — entirely mundane except for one wall of disturbing fetishes
two witches, teacher and apprentice — two beds, duplicate benches, bickering notes everywhere

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: very low (0.15-0.25) — cauldron glow and candles

anti_patterns: do not make it look like a wizard's tower — this is folk magic, not academic
no heavy masonry or formal library — shelves are rough, books are few and hand-written
ceiling hangs are decorative in description, not prop-placed on the floor
